title: R7RS final-mile: raise-continuable, error-object? family, exact-integer-sqrt, char-ci=? family
goal: Close the four genuinely-missing R7RS-small items called out in docs/breakdown/SCHEME_COMPATIBILITY.md. Each is a small focused implementation with a corresponding regression test. After this lands, Eshkol claims true R7RS-small parity (up from the current 95% / 232 of 244 procedures).
next_actions: raise-continuable (R7RS 6.11): extend the exception machinery so a handler invoked via with-exception-handler can return a value that becomes the result of the raise call. Currently raise unwinds; raise-continuable should not unwind on handler return.

error-object? / error-object-message / error-object-irritants (R7RS 6.11): wrap raised values in a tagged error-object struct (HEAP_SUBTYPE_ERROR_OBJECT) with message + irritants list. (error msg . irritants) constructs one; (raise <other-value>) does not wrap.
exact-integer-sqrt (R7RS 6.2.6): Newton iteration on bignums. Returns (values q r) where n = q*q + r and r < 2q+1. The numeric tower already handles bignum; just need the algorithm.

char-ci=? / char-ci<? / char-ci>? / char-ci<=? / char-ci>=? / string-ci=? / string-ci<? / string-ci>? / string-ci<=? / string-ci>=? (R7RS 6.6 + 6.7): fold-case + compare. char-foldcase already exists; build the predicates on top.
Update docs/breakdown/SCHEME_COMPATIBILITY.md's 'Known Limitations' table — flip each row from Missing to Supported, remove the workaround column.
acceptance: (raise-continuable 'err) inside a handler that returns 'recovered yields 'recovered (not exception propagation).
(exact-integer-sqrt (expt 10 50)) returns the exact square root + zero remainder.
docs/breakdown/SCHEME_COMPATIBILITY.md no longer lists these in the Missing column.
ux_payoff: Lets Eshkol claim TRUE R7RS-small parity (244/244 procedures). Removes a recurring sharp edge: every Scheme programmer who reaches for error-object-message finds it missing today.
